title: rosa canina var. frondosa
intro: Rosa canina var. frondosa, also known as the dwarf wild rose or Scotch rose, is a compact shrub variant of the common wild rose, characterized by its small size and distinctive foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its rich content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and salicylic acid, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Rosa canina var. frondosa include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and promote wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of Rosa canina var. frondosa include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
